text:
have shown
that certain sweeteners or artificial sugars (e.g.:
aspartame, E951) increase the risk of
cancer. Yet they are widely used in
drinks, sodas, desserts, chewing gum and
sugar-free candies.
It has also been shown that some food
additives damage or disturb the
gut microbiota and that they play a role
in the onset and worsening of Crohn's
disease or ulcerative colitis.
This is the case for emulsifiers: they are used to
mix elements that do not naturally mix (like water and oil),
improve texture, prolong shelf life
and give a soft texture.
FOOD
ADDITIVES
Food additives are added to foods to "improve" the product: so that it
is more colorful, more flavorful and/or keeps longer. They are often
listed with an E followed by a number (for example: E330 is citric acid,
naturally present in lemons). The law requires manufacturers to list them on the
ingredient list and they are often at the end of the list.
